name: Finnmark (Norway, 1967-2020)
description: A black field charged with a gold heraldic figure.
history: Finnmark was the northernmost county (fylke) of Norway. Its flag was the heraldic banner of its county coat of arms. The flag was used from 1967 until 1 January 2020, when Finnmark was merged with Troms to form Troms og Finnmark county. The merger was reversed in 2024, with Finnmark re-established under a new arms.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the former Finnmark county arms: a stylised gold figure on a black field
the central heraldic figure characteristic of Finnmark's former county arms
